mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and real retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change multiplied by RSAFS change) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained capacity expansion, rising industrial production leads to margin compression via increased competition, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising consumer confidence and credit availability, which positively impacts construction-related equities and broad market sentiment, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The monthly change in HOUST shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across all available data points.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to suppress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or show no significant decline when FEDFUNDS sustains levels above 2.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising payroll employment and accelerating CPI indicates wage-price spiral dynamics that force aggressive monetary tightening, which historically suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and accelerating CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns in out-of-sample periods (Rank IC > 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, a rapid rebound in real retail sales signals pent-up demand fulfillment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions turn sharply positive.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth rates during post-shock recoveries do not produce statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.96).
